figures_detected.description: Underside view of engine area showing front tube and splashguard bolt locations (indicated with arrows)
Tool KV10111100 being inserted between cylinder block and oil pan
Tool KV10111100 being tapped with hammer to slide and remove oil pan
Oil pan bottom view showing front and rear seal positions (labeled FRONT and REAR) with length dimension markers
Oil pan mating surface diagram showing liquid gasket bead application dimensions: 20 (0.79) mm at front corners and 3.5-4.5 (0.138-0.177) mm bead width

steps.text: Remove splashguards for access to oil pan area.

Remove oil pan bolts.
(1) Insert Tool between cylinder block and oil pan.
warnings: Be careful not to damage aluminum mating surface.
Do not insert screwdriver, or oil pan flange will be deformed.

steps.text: (2) Slide Tool by tapping its side with a hammer, and remove oil pan.
Before installing oil pan, remove all traces of liquid gasket from mating surfaces using a scraper. Also remove traces of liquid gasket from mating surface of cylinder block.

Fit new seals as shown. Ensure correct length, front & rear:
Length "ℓ":
  Front seal
    134 mm (5.28 in)
  Rear seal
    123 mm (4.84 in)
Apply a continuous bead of liquid gasket to mating surface of oil pan as shown.
notes: Use Genuine Liquid Gasket or equivalent.
Be sure liquid gasket is 3.5 to 4.5 mm (0.138 to 0.177 in) wide.

items.label: Liquid gasket bead width
items.condition: Applied to oil pan mating surface

items.label: Liquid gasket bead position from front corners
items.condition: Applied to oil pan mating surface

tools_referenced.name: Oil Pan Removal Tool
tools_referenced.usage: Insert between cylinder block and oil pan, then tap with hammer to slide and remove oil pan without damaging aluminum mating surface
